title: Decision Log
description: Audit trail of every meaningful choice made during production. Prevents hidden system behavior, enables reviewer inspection, and guards against context drift.

decision_id.description: Unique ID, e.g., d-001, d-002. Real runs also commonly use a bare 'id' field for this — kept as an additional property (see additionalProperties below) rather than forced to this exact name.

stage.description: Pipeline stage where this decision was made

category.description: What class of decision this is, e.g. pipeline_selection, provider_selection, renderer_family_selection, render_runtime_selection, composition_mode, playbook_selection, fallback_decision, budget_tradeoff, downgrade_approval, music_source, motion_commitment, concept_selection, voice_selection, capability_extension, playbook_override, visual_accuracy_check. Not a closed enum: real agent runs routinely coin new, equally legitimate categories (e.g. capability_constraint, creative_direction, source_mode) as new kinds of decisions arise.

subject.description: What is being decided, e.g., 'TTS provider for narration'

oneOf.description: Shorthand: just the option's label/id when no per-option scoring was done.

reason.description: Why this option scored this way

options_considered.description: All options evaluated (including rejected ones). Real runs range from a bare list of option labels to fully-scored option objects — both are accepted.

selected.description: option_id of the selected option. Real runs commonly use 'recommendation' instead — kept as an additional property rather than forced to this exact name.

reason.description: Why this option was selected over alternatives. Real runs commonly use 'recommendation_rationale', 'rationale', or 'notes' instead — kept as additional properties rather than forced to this exact name.

user_visible.description: Whether this decision was surfaced to the user

user_approved.description: Whether the user explicitly approved this decision

confidence.description: Agent's confidence in this decision (0 = uncertain, 1 = certain)
